gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/581)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/581)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ
أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُتَرِّبُ الْكِتَابَ وَ

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> that he<sup>asws</sup> would (sprinkle) dust upon the letter (to dry the ink), and said: ‘There is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 8. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan al-Rida (a.s.) would dry up the ink on a newlywritten letter by means of placing soil on it and would say that doing so is not an offense.”
